zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Harcourt於1379年在英國斯塔福爾薩爾頓受洗時，他的父親Sir Thomas Harcourt37歲，母親Maud Grey34歲。他於1405年5月2日與Margaret Lutley在英國英格蘭奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1431年在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Harcourt于1379年在英国斯塔福爾薩爾頓受洗时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Harcourt37岁，母亲Maud Grey34岁。他于1405年5月2日与Margaret Lutley在英国英格兰奧可福爾斯坦頓 聖 約翰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1431年在英国奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harcourt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the places in Eure and Calvados named Harcourt, from Old French cour(t) ‘court, farmyard’ (see Court ) with an obscure first element. This surname is very rare in France.

English: habitational name from either of two places in Shropshire named Harcourt. The one near Cleobury Mortimer gets the name from Old English heafocere ‘hawker, falconer’ + cot ‘hut, cottage’; the one near Wem has as its first element Old English hearpere (see Harper ).

History: The surname of French origin (see 1 above) is listed along with the equally rare or even extinct variants or altered forms Haraucourt, Herancourt, and Héraucourt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Court
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name from Middle English curt, court ‘large house’ (a word of the same ultimately Latin origin as 2 below), denoting someone who lived or worked in a manor house or castle, a courtly retainer.
French: topographic name from Old French co(u)rt ‘court, farmyard’ (from Latin cohors, cors, genitive cohortis, cortis ‘court, farmyard, enclosure’).

English and French: nickname for a physically small man, or ironically for a large man, from the adjective Middle English curt, Old French co(u)rt ‘short, small’ (from Latin curtus ‘curtailed, truncated, cut short, broken off’). Compare French Lecourt .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Harcourt was christened in 1379, in Saredon, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas Harcourt, was 37 and his mother, Maud Grey, was 34. He married Margaret Lutley on 2 May 1405, in Stanton St John, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1431, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 53.

ko.Stanton, Oxfordshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Harcourt(이)가 1379년 영국 스태퍼드실르 사레던에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Harcourt(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Grey(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1405년 5월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 스트 존에서 Margaret Lutley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1431년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Stanton, Oxfordshire, England: стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Harcourt был крещен в 1379 г. в местности саредон, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Harcourt было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Grey, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Lutley 2 мая 1405 г. в местности стантон ст джон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1431 г. в местности стантон харкоурт, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет.
